validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile exits 0 on dispatch_batch.py + merge_batch.py + tests/test_batch_logs.py (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_batch_logs -v passes 6/7 with 1 OS-specific skip (2.9s); pre-existing test_dispatch_batch + test_merge_batch still pass 31/31; python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 221/222 with 1 skip (40.2s, up from 215 -- 7 new cases for unit_043)

checkpoint.summary: unit_043 complete. Log-directory creation logic landed in dispatch_batch.py and merge_batch.py with best-effort semantics at both the helper AND the call site. dispatch_batch writes .harness/logs/<batch_id>/batch.json (batch plan + dispatched fleet + ISO timestamp) after the fleet is built. merge_batch writes merge.log (grep-friendly: batch_id/outcome/merged/conflicted/skipped with one '- <unit_id>' bullet per entry) and validation.log (validator_ok: True|False + the validator's evidence string) -- both wrapped in try/except Exception, and validation.log writes BEFORE the validation-failure rollback branch so a failed batch still leaves evidence on disk. Empty-fleet short-circuit writes NO logs (nothing ran to log -- the absence is itself the reported fact). New test_batch_logs.py (290 LOC, 7 cases, 1 OS-specific skip): happy-path batch.json, happy-path merge.log + validation.log, validation-failure still writes both logs, empty-fleet writes no log, helpers swallow OSError on read-only dirs (Linux/Mac) or via empty batch_id guard (cross-platform), and -- crucially -- dispatch_batch survives when the helper is monkeypatched to always raise OSError (proves the call-site try/except is load-bearing, not just the helper's internal swallow). sub-agent <unit_id>.md summaries remain sub-agent-owned per the harness-unit contract; no orchestrator code needed. .harness/.gitignore already carries logs/ from harness creation. Suite 215 -> 222 (221 + 1 Windows skip). First Windows-aware skip in the suite -- the skip message points at the equivalent cross-platform coverage for substance traceability.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_044 (PHASE_010): integration test that runs a sample batch end-to-end (compute_parallel_batch -> dispatch_batch -> fake sub-agent commits -> merge_batch) and asserts ALL four log artifacts exist with non-empty content: batch.json (JSON-decodable), merge.log (contains expected outcome + unit_ids), validation.log (contains validator message), AND <unit_id>.md (sub-agent summary). The sub-agent summary is nominally agent-written; for this test, fabricate one inline as the sub-agent would. Best place to put it: extend tests/integration/test_invoke_rewrite.py with a new class TestBatchLogArtifactsEndToEnd, since that module already has the two-fixture infrastructure. Validation: the integration test exits 0; full suite still green.
